turnResults.user: We are starting the Green Haven Community Garden in West Philadelphia. Our goal is to serve local residents by reducing food insecurity and providing a green space for community building.
turnResults.reply: That sounds like a wonderful project for the neighborhood. Thinking about the long term, what is the ultimate change you hope the Green Haven Community Garden will bring to West Philadelphia?
failures: reply did not contain any of [captured, resources]

turnResults.user: We have two part-time coordinators, $5,000 in seed grants, donated tools from the city, and a partnership with a local horticulture expert for training.
turnResults.reply: Thanks for sharing that. It sounds like you have a strong start with staff, funding, and tools. What about the physical space for the garden itself?
failures: reply did not contain any of [activities]

turnResults.user: We hold weekly planting workshops, maintain 20 raised beds, and run a bi-weekly harvest distribution stand.
turnResults.reply: Those sound like the central activities for the garden. Thinking about the bi-weekly harvest distribution stand, about how many residents do you expect to reach?
failures: reply did not contain any of [quality, fidelity]

turnResults.user: We maintain quality by using organic-only soil standards. For fidelity, we use a weekly checklist to ensure every bed is watered and weeded according to the garden manual.
turnResults.reply: That's a great system for keeping the garden healthy and on track. You mentioned the weekly planting workshops—about how many people are you expecting to attend those?
failures: reply did not contain any of [outcomes]

turnResults.user: Short term, we want residents to learn basic gardening. Medium term, we expect 200lbs of produce per season. Long term, we hope to see a 10% decrease in reported food insecurity among regular members.
turnResults.reply: That's a great progression from learning new skills to seeing a real change in food security. Thinking about the 200lbs of produce, is that the total amount you're aiming to grow and distribute each season?
failures: reply did not contain any of [summary, complete]

turnResults.user: We work with high schoolers in the city to help them get jobs.
turnResults.reply: That sounds like really important work. If the program is successful, what's the long-term change you hope to see for those students and for the city?

turnResults.reply: That's a great, clear goal. For a student to be ready to enroll, what would they need to have accomplished by the time they graduate from high school?

results.description: All-in-one impact statement is captured and confirmed.

turnResults.user: We serve middle school students in North Philadelphia and our long-term goal is that they read on grade level and transition successfully to high school.
turnResults.reply:
That's a great foundation. Ensuring students are reading on grade level and prepared for high school is so important.

Thinking about the bigger picture, what kind of long-term change in the community do you hope this contributes to?

turnResults.user: Yes, that captures it.
turnResults.reply: Got it. To make all of that happen for your students, what does your team need in terms of staffing or key materials to run the program?

results.description: When user provides a resource list, the patch captures multiple resource buckets.

turnResults.user: We have program staff, volunteers, partner counselors, curriculum materials, laptops, grant funding, and staff training.
turnResults.reply: Thanks, that's a very clear list of resources. Now, what are the main activities that your staff and volunteers carry out as part of the program?

results.description: Resource response should not be ignored across turns and should keep resources in model state.

turnResults.user: Staff mentors, school partners, donated laptops, grant funding, and evidence-based training.
turnResults.reply:
Thanks, that's a helpful list of resources.

Now, thinking about the big picture, what is the ultimate change you hope your program will create for the people or community you serve?

turnResults.user: Anything else you need from me on resources?
turnResults.reply:
That's a great list to start with, thank you. We can always come back and add more detail to the resources later on.

For now, could you tell me about that ultimate change you hope your program will create?
